summary: Phase 3.0 (Planning Gate of Phase 3 — Entity Model, Turn Loop, Combat) complete. Decision memo authored at artifacts/decision-memo-phase-3.md with 16 numbered decisions covering algorithm (player-tick → resolve → monsters in ascending-id order), entity model (integer ids, integer-only attributes), action-type vocabulary (wait | move | attack | descend), per-roll combat formula (sha256(stateHashPre || encodeAction(action) || anchor || lp(domain) || u32_le(index))), RunState in-memory shape, per-floor sim sub-stream (streams.simFloor(floorN)), spawn placement, FSM monster AI, stream-isolation lint + runtime guards, headless playthrough harness, diagnostic-page extension, death/boss-kill transitions, monster registry (~6 kinds + boss), item registry (data-only), 3.0/3.A/3.B phase split, two new self-tests (sim-cross-runtime-digest + sim-stream-isolation), and Phase 2.A carry-forward folding (N3 gen-fixtures test + N7 decoder relocation; N6 stays deferred). Architecture-red-team review at artifacts/red-team-phase-3.md identified six blocking issues (B1 lp_utf8(domain) ambiguity + missing pre-image domain anchor, B2 implicit rollU32 byte order + bit-extraction recipe, B3 ai:tiebreak registry entry contradicts no-PRNG-AI claim, B4 simFloor(floorN) range-validation + non-collision proof missing, B5 per-tick __consumed delta semantic on descend wrong as written, B6 N7 decoder relocation under-specified test/coverage scope) plus ten non-blocking concerns; all six blockers and at least the named non-blockers (N3 lint scope, N4 direction coordinate system, N5 BFS distance metric, N6 spawn determinism, N7 HP underflow clamp) are addressed in an in-memo addendum that overrides any conflicting prose. Follow-up architecture-red-team review of the addendum returned APPROVE WITH NITS — addendum cleanly resolves all blockers and introduces only implementer-side concerns (uniformIndex pseudocode uses banned Math.floor/divide and must be re-expressed in integer-only ops; parseFloor: error-message prefix in relocated decoder needs renaming to decodeBase64Url:; idiv helper home decision deferred to Phase 3.A; harness-imports-mapgen Phase 5 carry-forward note worth tracking). No application code is shipped in Phase 3.0 (correct posture for a planning gate); the Phase 2.A carry-forwards (N3 gen-fixtures test, N7 decoder relocation) land as the first commits of Phase 3.A's drift-detection sweep, before any net-new src/sim/** code. Phase 3.A implementation may begin in the next phase cycle.
suggested_commit_message: phase-3.0: planning gate (decision memo + red-team + addendum resolving B1-B6)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 2 (2.0 + 2.A + 2.B) foundation has not drifted before opening Phase 3 work.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: Last verified locally at HEAD of phase-2.A — 411 passed (vitest run --coverage); 100% lines / 100% statements / 100% functions on src/core/**, src/mapgen/**, src/registries/**. Phase 3.0 ships no application code, so this baseline is unchanged; the next CI run on the Phase 3.0 commit will reconfirm before Phase 3.A begins.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors at HEAD of phase-2.A (CI run 25294232381). Phase 3.0 only edits artifacts/decision-memo-phase-3.md; lint scope is unaffected.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors at HEAD of phase-2.A (CI run 25294232381). Phase 3.0 only edits artifacts/decision-memo-phase-3.md; typecheck scope is unaffected.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Phase 3.0 ships no user-facing code (planning gate posture); the primary workflow remains the live https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ diagnostic page with the Phase 2.A floor-preview UI, last verified green in commit 394801f per Phase 2.B approval (artifacts/phase-approval.json before this update; full evidence at the prior approval's verification block including HTTP 200 + Playwright cross-runtime green on chromium/firefox/webkit). Phase 3.A will extend the diagnostic page with a scripted-playthrough button (memo decision 10); Phase 3.B will re-verify it live.

planning_gate.deferred_question_surfaced_in_addendum: Whether mod-bearing FingerprintInputs are exercised by Phase 3 — explicitly deferred to Phase 8 with mods: [] -only test cases in Phase 3.
red_team_followup_nits: Decision N6's uniformIndex pseudocode uses Math.floor and the / operator; both are banned by src/sim/**'s no-float-arithmetic rule (eslint-rules/no-float-arithmetic.cjs:13,58). Phase 3.A implementation must translate to integer-only ops (e.g. (0xFFFFFFFF - (0xFFFFFFFF % n)) for the rejection bound). The lint message references an idiv helper at sim/math; the helper's home (src/sim/math.ts vs src/core/math.ts) is a Phase 3.A decision.

After the N7 decoder relocation (decision 16, B6 resolution), the parseFloor: error-message prefix at /workspace/src/mapgen/serialize.ts:409,418 inside decodeBase64Url is no longer accurate (the function will live in src/core/hash.ts and is not part of parseFloor). Phase 3.A must rename to decodeBase64Url: as part of the move.

src/sim/harness.ts's permission to import generateFloor from src/mapgen/index.ts (B5 resolution) is a single legal sim→mapgen edge introduced for Phase 3 only. Phase 5's input-driven loop is expected to relocate this orchestration to src/run/loop.ts (or equivalent), at which point the eslint exception goes away. Track this as a Phase 5 carry-forward so the exception does not ossify.

BFS allocation per monster tick is permitted by the addendum's per-tick __consumed-empty invariant (which constrains stream usage, not heap allocation). Allocating a width×height distance map per player tick on a 60×24 floor is ~3KB; modest but not zero. Worth flagging on the diagnostic page if Phase 7 adds a multi-monster boss arena where BFS becomes a hot loop. Not a Phase 3 blocker.

Anchor spoofing was probed: 'icefall:roll:v1:' literal cannot collide with any reachable encodeAction tail under Phase 1's tag space (0x10/0x20/0x30, with type field at the front). Future tag (0x40+) accepting raw user-controlled bytes still cannot spoof the anchor because the length-prefix immediately preceding the anchor differs between roll pre-image and action-only pre-image. Not blocking under Phase 3's threat model; revisit when Phase 8 broadens the threat model to external action logs.
planning_gate.code_review_status: Phase 3.0 ships no application code (planning gate posture); the deliverable is the decision memo + red-team review + addendum to the decision memo. The follow-up architecture-red-team review of the addendum (verdict APPROVE WITH NITS) is the equivalent gate for a no-code planning phase; a code-reviewer reinvocation would have nothing application-side to review. Phase 2.0 had carry-forward Phase 1 implementation (no-float-arithmetic invalid-fixture expansion + encodeAction tag-order combinatoric test) which justified a code-reviewer pass; Phase 3.0 has no equivalent because the Phase 2.A carry-forwards (N3 gen-fixtures test, N7 decoder relocation) land in Phase 3.A's drift-detection sweep, not in 3.0.

from_phase_2_A.code_review_N3: Add focused unit test for tools/gen-fixtures.ts covering slug rejection on non-alphanumeric input, generatePair happy path, readManifest malformed input. Approximate size 30-50 lines of test. Lands as the second commit of Phase 3.A's drift-detection sweep (after N7 below).
from_phase_2_A.code_review_N7: Relocate decodeBase64Url and B64URL_REVERSE from src/mapgen/serialize.ts to src/core/hash.ts (alongside base64url encoder). Per addendum B6: re-export from serialize.ts so fixture-pack round-trip tests pass unchanged; add tests/core/hash.decodeBase64Url.test.ts covering r mod 4 ∈ {0,1,2,3} branches + invalid-character path + high-codepoint path + empty-string round-trip + 50-seed property round-trip; lift 100% line/branch/function coverage on the relocated function onto src/core/** gate; verify src/core/** ESLint scope passes the relocated implementation; preserve the (c: number) type annotation on the lookup closure. Lands as the first commit of Phase 3.A's drift-detection sweep, before any net-new src/sim/** code.
from_phase_2_A.code_review_N6_deferred: Door-placement coverage on standard floors stays deferred (frozen by fixture pack; revisit in Phase 7 when boss-arena door semantics get reworked).

from_phase_3_0_addendum_red_team_followu.uniform_index_integer_only: Decision N6's uniformIndex must be expressed in integer-only ops; pseudocode in the addendum uses Math.floor and / which are banned. Implementer's choice of integer-division idiom (or a new src/core/math.idiv helper) lands in Phase 3.A.
from_phase_3_0_addendum_red_team_followu.decode_error_prefix_rename: Rename parseFloor: error-message prefix to decodeBase64Url: in the relocated function (src/core/hash.ts after relocation).
from_phase_3_0_addendum_red_team_followu.harness_mapgen_exception_carry_to_phase_: Phase 5 carry-forward — when the input-driven loop replaces runScripted, relocate the harness's generateFloor + spawnFloorEntities orchestration to src/run/loop.ts (or equivalent) and remove the eslint single-file exception for src/sim/harness.ts. Add to the Phase 5 deliverable list when Phase 5 planning begins.

phases_md_split.note: docs/PHASES.md already documents Phase 3 as a planning-gate phase in the planning-gates list at line 7 ('phases 1.A, 2, 3, 4, and 8'). Phase 3 has not been formally split into 3.0 / 3.A / 3.B in PHASES.md the way Phase 2 was — that split is documented in the decision memo (decision 14) and is treated as the operative split for Phase 3 execution. PHASES.md line-level edits to formalize the 3.0/3.A/3.B partition are deferred to a Phase 3.A documentation commit so this 3.0 commit stays narrowly scoped to the planning artifacts.
phase_3_0_acceptance_criteria_recorded_i: decision-memo-phase-3.md exists and was reviewed by architecture-red-team before any Phase 3 implementation code is written
red-team review at artifacts/red-team-phase-3.md exists; all six blocking issues are addressed via an in-memo addendum that supersedes the original prose
follow-up architecture-red-team review of the addendum confirms blocker resolution

next_phase: Phase 3.A — Entity Model, Turn Loop, Combat (sandbox-verifiable). First three commits per addendum sequencing: (1) N7 decoder relocation with focused tests, (2) N3 gen-fixtures.ts unit test, (3) docs/ARCHITECTURE.md update referencing Phase 3 frozen contracts (items 8-13 in addendum) + new streams.simFloor(floorN) accessor + lint-scope additions + deferred contracts (one-way descent, verifier trailing-after-terminal). Subsequent commits implement src/sim/** (entity model, turn loop, combat), src/registries/monsters.ts and src/registries/items.ts, src/core/streams.ts simFloor extension, eslint.config.js lint additions, src/core/self-test.ts SIM_DIGEST + sim-stream-isolation checks, and the diagnostic-page scripted-playthrough section. Phase 3.A acceptance gate: npm ci && npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build && npm run test:e2e all green inside the sandbox + 100% line/branch/function coverage on src/core/**, src/mapgen/**, src/sim/**, src/registries/**. Phase 3.B (live verification, post-push) is a separate phase per decision 14.
